title: Sanguine-Pearson Group Relationship Mapping
description: A visual mapping showing how elements from two distinct groups (Sanguine and Pearson) relate to each other through a specific mathematical operation or property, demonstrating concepts like homomorphism or isomorphism in group theory.
relationships: f is a homomorphism mapping elements from Sanguine Group to Pearson Group
f(a * b) = f(a) • f(b) for all a,b in G, preserving group operation
